title: machaeranthera pygmaea
intro: Machaeranthera pygmaea, also known as Pygmy False Goldenrod, is a small flowering plant native to the southwestern United States. It is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its essential oils and flavonoids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved respiratory function, and enhanced immune system response. Machaeranthera pygmaea can be prepared for medicinal use through tea infusions, tinctures, or infusions of the dried flowers and leaves, which should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
